'''Chris Flannery''' (born 5 June 1980) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s.

A [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] representative forward, he played his club football in the [[National Rugby League]] for the [[Sydney Roosters]], with whom he won the [[2002 NRL Premiership]], and in the [[Super League]] for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], with whom he won the [[2008 Challenge Cup]].

Flannery's usual position was {{rlp|lk}} although he also played as a {{rlp|sr|so}} or {{rlp|ce}}.<ref name="Player Profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/player/59|title=Player Profile|publisher=Saints|date=2007-07-03|accessdate=2007-07-03}}</ref> After retiring Flannery administered the [[Sunshine Coast Falcons]] of the [[Queensland Cup]].

==Background==
Born in [[Cowra, New South Wales]], Flannery's family moved to the [[Sunshine Coast, Queensland]] when he was 10, where he played his junior rugby league for the Kawana Dolphins.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2020/07/05/we-all-like-to-think-we-started-the-streak-all-the-2006-team/|title='We all like to think we started the streak, all the 2006 team'|date=5 July 2020|website=QRL}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===National Rugby League===
{{Unreferencedsect|date=May 2025}}
Flannery made his National Rugby League début for the Sydney Roosters in Round 14 of the [[2000 NRL season]] against the [[Wests Tigers]] at [[Campbelltown Stadium]] on 7 May. He was first selected to play for [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] in Game II of the [[2002 State of Origin series]] at [[Queensland Sport and Athletics Centre|ANZ Stadium in Brisbane]] on 5 June. Queensland won 26–18.

Flannery played for the Roosters from the interchange bench in their [[2002 NRL grand final]] victory over the [[New Zealand Warriors]], scoring a [[Try (rugby)|try]]. Having won the [[2002 NRL Premiership]], the Roosters traveled to England to play the [[2003 World Club Challenge]] against [[Super League]] champions, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. Flannery played at centre in Sydney's victory. He again played from the interchange bench for the Roosters in the [[2003 NRL grand final]] which was lost to the [[Penrith Panthers]].

Flannery played for Queensland in all three matches of the [[2004 State of Origin series]]. He played for the Roosters at lock forward in their [[2004 NRL grand final]] loss to cross-Sydney rivals, [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]].

===Super League===
In 2007, Flannery signed for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] on a three-and-a-half-year deal.<ref name="Saints sign Australian Flannery">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/st_helens/6752737.stm|title=Saints sign Australian Flannery|publisher=BBC|date=2007-06-14|access-date=2007-06-14}}</ref><ref name="Saints Scoop Flannery">{{cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/news/340|title=Saints Scoop Flannery|publisher=Saints|date=2007-06-14|accessdate=2007-06-14}}</ref> Flannery was brought in as a direct replacement for fellow Australian [[Jason Hooper]] who had to retire following problems with his shoulder.<ref name="Flannery - joined St Helens">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/06/14/RUGBYL_St_Helens.html|title=Flannery - joined St Helens|publisher=Sportinglife|date=2007-06-14|accessdate=2007-06-14}}</ref> Flannery described himself as a hard worker and strong in defence. "I also like to add a bit to the ball playing by taking it up at first or second receiver. I also like to run good lines into holes."<ref name="Flannery full of anticipation">{{cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/news/366|title=Flannery full of anticipation|publisher=SaintsRLFC.com|date=2007-06-25|accessdate=2007-06-26}}</ref> He commenced his [[Knowsley Road]] career in July, 2007, after recovering from a broken leg. He played in the [[2008 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref name="2008 Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|title=2008 Grand Final|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-04|access-date=2008-10-05}}</ref><ref name="Low maintenance Flanno is an asset to Saints squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/9139243.Low_maintenance_Flanno_is_an_asset_to_Saints_squad/|title=Low maintenance Flanno is an asset to Saints squad|work=St Helens Star|date=14 July 2011|accessdate=30 November 2017}}</ref>

He played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=AFP|title=Leeds makes it Super League hat-trick|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2009-10-11/leeds-makes-it-super-league-hat-trick/1098564|accessdate=22 November 2019|newspaper=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation|ABC]] News|date=11 October 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Linfoot|first=Ben|title=Grand Final: Leeds Rhinos 18 St Helens 10|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_commentary/0,19936,11070_3204177,00.html|accessdate=22 November 2019|newspaper=Sky Sports|date=10 October 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091206112549/http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_commentary/0%2C19936%2C11070_3204177%2C00.html|archive-date=6 December 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}}</ref>

==Post-playing==
After returning to Australia, Flannery became chief executive of [[Queensland Cup]] team the [[Sunshine Coast Falcons]].<ref>{{cite news|last1=Taylor|first1=Steele|title=Falcons feeling confident about NRL club link|url=http://www.sunshinecoastdaily.com.au/news/falcons-feeling-confident-about-nrl-club-link/2306473/|accessdate=2 July 2014|work=Sunshine Coast Daily|publisher=APN|date=2 July 2014}}</ref>
Flannery was inducted into the Sunshine Coast Sports Hall of Fame in 2023.<ref>{{cite news|title=Rugby league player inducted into Sports Hall of Fame|url=https://www.usc.edu.au/about/unisc-news/news-archive/2023/january/rugby-league-player-inducted-into-sports-hall-of-fame|newspaper=University of the Sunshine Coast|accessdate=16 May 2025}}</ref>
